description: The figure presents true stress and strain hardening rate versus true strain curves obtained from room temperature tensile tests for various Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels, illustrating distinct deformation mechanisms. Six curves are plotted for specific compositions: Fe-22Mn-0.6C (dashed line, labeled TWIP) showing the characteristic "hump" associated with deformation twinning and strong microstructure refinement; Fe-30Mn-2Al-1.2C (dotted line) exhibiting multi-stage strain hardening linked to dislocation substructure refinement at lower strains and twinning at higher strains; Fe-30Mn-8Al-1.2C (solid line, labeled DSBR), Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C (red line, labeled MBIP), and Fe-27Mn-12Al-0.9C (blue line, labeled SIP) demonstrating qualitatively similar strain dependence to TWIP steels despite different microstructural mechanisms involving planar glide affected by SROs or κ′-carbides; and Fe-15Mn-9Al-0.7C (black dashed line) showing anomalous monotonic decay of strain hardening rate with increasing strain. The x-axis spans true strain from 0 to 0.8, while the y-axis spans true stress/strain hardening rate from 0 to 3000 MPa. The figure establishes that high-Al content (>7%) austenitic steels suppress mechanical twinning and activate newly discovered plasticity mechanisms (MBIP, DSBR, SIP), though these cannot explain negative strain hardening rates observed in highly alloyed or peak-aged conditions.
